items.title: Engagement ratio before k-NN segmentation
items.scenario: A product team engineers `sessions_per_week` (0–15) and `revenue_per_session` ($2–$800) from raw event logs. A k-NN segmenter without a scaler assigns neighbors almost entirely by dollar magnitude. A `ColumnTransformer` numeric branch with `StandardScaler` after imputation lets both engineered signals influence distance.
items.explanation: Engineered ratios can still have wildly different ranges; distance-based models need a scale step after feature construction, not just after raw load.

items.title: Log revenue + Ridge on mixed units
items.scenario: Feature engineering adds `log1p(monthly_revenue)` and `tenure_months`. Ridge without scaling shrinks the log-revenue coefficient artificially because its variance differs from tenure. The training pipeline applies `StandardScaler` on the numeric branch only, leaving one-hot `region` columns unscaled.
items.explanation: Even after log transforms, column variance may differ; regularized linear models need comparable scales on the continuous engineered branch.

items.title: Tree model on engineered counts — scaler omitted
items.scenario: A fraud team engineers `txn_count_7d` and `amount_to_limit_ratio` and trains `HistGradientBoostingClassifier` with no scaler in the numeric branch. Tree splits on thresholds behave the same if amounts are multiplied by 1000, so the team skips scaling and focuses on encoding `merchant_category`.
items.explanation: Algorithm sensitivity matters: pure tree pipelines on engineered numerics rarely need a scaler for correctness.

items.title: Mixed pipeline: embeddings + k-NN reranker
items.scenario: A search team stores 128-d embedding features (already unit-normalized) alongside engineered `popularity_score` (0–1e6). A k-NN reranker uses `ColumnTransformer` to StandardScaler only the popularity column while passing embeddings through unchanged, preventing raw score magnitude from swamping vector distance.
items.explanation: Not every column needs the same transform—branch-level scaling targets columns whose engineered scale would distort distance.
